mnemonics: CIA: Create, Initialize, Assign - The three-step protocol for initializing a structure.
Blank Slate Blues: A struct without initialization is like a blank slate - you never know what garbage (old data) is written on it.
keyPoints: Structure initialization assigns initial values to its members at the time of declaration.
An uninitialized structure contains garbage values in its members, which can lead to undefined behavior.
Initialization can be done using an initializer list `{ }` or by assigning values to each member individually after declaration.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to initialize a structure, leading to program errors from garbage values.
Providing fewer values in the initializer list than there are members, leaving the remaining members uninitialized.
Using an assignment operator `=` instead of the initializer syntax when declaring a struct, e.g., `struct Point p1 = (1, 2);` instead of `struct Point p1 = {1, 2};`.
